subject: math
topic_title: WORD PROBLEMS LEADING TO LINEAR AND QUADRATIC EQUATIONS
learning_objectives: Understand word problems leading to linear and quadratic equations

sections.heading: Concept
sections.content:
Example
The product of two numbers is 12. The sum of the larger number and twice the smaller number is 11. Find the two numbers.
Solution
Let x = the larger number
y = the smaller number
Product, x y = 12 …………….eq1
From the last statement,
x + 2y = 11 ………….. eq2
From eq2, x = 11 – 2y …………...eq3
Sub. Into eq1
y(11 – 2y) = 12
11y – 2y2 = 12
2y2 -11y + 12 = 0
2y2 – 8y – 3y + 12 = 0
2y(y-4) – 3(y-4) = 0
(2y-3)(y-4) =0
2y-3 =0 or y-4 =0
2y = 3 or y = 4
y= 3/2 or 4
when y = 3/2 when y=4
x = 11 – 2y x = 11- 2y
x = 11 – 2(3/2) x = 11 – 2(4)
x = 11 – 3 x = 11 – 8
x = 8 x = 3
Therefore, (8 , 3/2)(3 , 4)

sections.heading: Examples and Practice
sections.content:
Evaluation
Solve the following simultaneous equation
1. (a) 22x-3y = 32, 3x-2y = 81 (b) 2x+2y=1, 32x+y = 27
2. Bisi’s and Fibie’s ages add up to 29. Seven years ago Bisi was twice as old as Fibie. Find their present ages.
